blockers.title: Conflict release can create rows that are still not claimable
blockers.evidence: `convex/admin/claims.ts:154-165` checks corrected-email collisions only against `importedMembers`, then `convex/admin/claims.ts:237-240` marks the row `unclaimed`. It never checks whether `members.by_email` already owns the target email. But the normal claim path returns before reading imported rows when a member already exists at that email (`convex/members.ts:615-620`, `convex/members.ts:677-683`). The UI then reports success as “Released. It can be claimed again” (`src/admin/ClaimConflictsQueue.tsx:153-154`), which is false for that case and violates spec criterion 2’s “released for claim through normal matchClaim” requirement.
blockers.required_fix: Before setting `claim_state: "unclaimed"`, validate the target email against both `members.by_email` and live `importedMembers` rows. Return a neutral collision/duplicate error if a member row already owns the email. Add regression tests for corrected-email and original-email collisions with existing members.

blockers.title: Corrected-email resolution can archive unreviewed duplicate siblings
blockers.evidence: When `correctedEmail` is supplied, `resolveConflictAsClaimed` gathers every other `conflict` row at the original email (`convex/admin/claims.ts:186-188`) and archives all of them in a loop (`convex/admin/claims.ts:218-235`). The vault/spec decision is pair-specific: “the other row of a duplicate-email pair” becomes the permanent trail (`specs/admin-panel.spec.md:117-129`). If more than one conflict row shares an email, this mutation can permanently park rows the admin did not explicitly identify as the non-matching pair.
blockers.required_fix: Make the archive target explicit or refuse automatic archive unless there is exactly one other conflict row at the original email. Add a regression for three same-email conflict rows proving unselected rows are not archived.

blockers.title: Claim-conflict query leaks full emails for conflicts and suppressed minors
blockers.evidence: Spec criterion 2 says `listConflicts` shows masked/partial identity, conflict reason, match signals, and days since change, “not the full row” (`specs/admin-panel.spec.md:108-111`). The query returns `normalized_email` for every `conflict`, `suppressed_minor`, and `archived_conflict` row (`convex/admin/claims.ts:35-42`, `convex/admin/claims.ts:64-68`), and the React client uses those raw emails for grouping (`src/admin/ClaimConflictsQueue.tsx:51-65`). That puts a bulk list of conflict and minor-held email addresses in the browser on every admin page load, beyond the masked review surface the spec describes.
blockers.required_fix: Do duplicate grouping server-side and return non-PII fields such as `liveDuplicateCount`, `sharesEmailWithOther`, or an opaque group id. If full contact email reveal is operationally required, add a separate audited per-row reveal flow or update the vault/spec explicitly.

non_blocking_notes: Targeted Vitest run could not start in this read-only review sandbox because Vite tried to write `node_modules/.vite-temp/...` and failed with EPERM.
Diff-added lines scan found no em dash/en dash. Secret scan found env-var names/placeholders and test keys only.

spec_compliance.evidence: Admin queue queries/mutations/actions call `requireSuperAdmin` or `requireSuperAdminInAction`; `amISuperAdmin` is only a UI courtesy boolean.
Core queue exists, but release is not guaranteed to produce a normally claimable row, auto-archive can overreach beyond the decided pair, and the query returns full emails.
Approval goes through shared `applyPipelineDecision`, which requires standard lane, active lifecycle, and latest true pipeline consent.
Admin surface exposes resend only; confirmation remains in the guardian token flow.
Submission creates inert `dataRequests` records; admin approval/rejection is gated and audited. Fulfil/export execution is absent per the spec’s 2026-07-04 deferral.
No diff-added em/en dashes found; no gold token usage in the added admin/member data UI.

Coverage exists, but it lacks regressions for existing-member email collisions, multi-sibling duplicate archive, and email leakage from `listConflicts`; full Vitest execution was blocked by the read-only sandbox.

summary: The slice has the main authorization gates and most queue mechanics, but the claim-conflict path still has data-integrity and privacy blockers. It can report a row as released when normal claiming cannot ever claim it, can archive more than the decided duplicate pair, and returns raw emails for conflict/minor-held rows despite the masked review-surface requirement.
